sanskrit: असुरीं योनिमापन्ना मूढा जन्मनि जन्मनि । मामप्राप्यैव कौन्तेय ततो यान्त्यधमां गतिम् ॥
hindiTranslation: हे कुन्तीनन्दन  वे मूढ मनुष्य मेरेको प्राप्त न करके ही जन्मजन्मान्तरमें आसुरी योनिको प्राप्त होते हैं, फिर उससे भी अधिक अधम गतिमें अर्थात् भयङ्कर नरकोंमें चले जाते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Those deluded beings, born again and again into shadowed births, never reach me and sink into a lower state.
essence: Repeated delusion keeps pulling a person farther from the divine.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: People trapped in delusion keep taking shadowed births again and again. Because they never turn toward the divine, they sink into still lower states.
simpleInsight: There is urgency in seeing how repeated confusion hardens into a downward pattern.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen between duty and grief. Krishna has just described the dark nature of destructive people, and now he shows the end of that path: repeated rebirth in shadowed conditions, with no rise toward him.
modernRelevance: You keep making the same self-sabotaging choice after the breakup, after the apology, after the warning. The pattern does not feel dramatic — until you notice it has become your life.
translationLiteral: असुरीम् (shadowed) / योनिम् (birth) / आपन्नाः (having entered) / मूढाः (deluded ones) / जन्मनि जन्मनि (birth after birth) / माम् (me) / अप्राप्य (not reaching) / एव (indeed) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / ततः (then) / यान्ति (go) / अधमाम् (lower) / गतिम् (state)
insight: It shifts the mind from treating repeated bad choices as isolated mistakes to seeing them as a reinforcing descent.

quoteVariants.text: Delusion repeats itself. Without reaching me, they fall into lower births.
What you refuse to reach keeps pulling you down.
Delusion repeats. Reach higher or fall lower.
